reasoning: The FRED statistical model gives a 26% probability of the real interest rate increasing above its most recent value of 1.748. This value is relatively high compared to recent months, making mean reversion likely. Additionally, web search reveals that the nominal 10-year Treasury yield has fallen significantly in recent days, from ~4.22% on February 6 to 4.04% on February 13, 2026 (its lowest level since November). Given that the Cleveland Fed's model utilizes nominal Treasury yields as a core input, this substantial ~18 basis point drop in nominal yields over the past week strongly suggests the real interest rate estimate for March 2026 will decrease compared to the February 2026 value, barring an even sharper collapse in inflation expectations. Therefore, I am adjusting the baseline 26% probability downwards to 18%.
